summary: Iteration 3 smoke tests passed for Business Profile save fix, Payments module (settings + record + UPI QR link), and INR (₹) currency across UI. Backend endpoints for business/profile, payments/settings, payments (create/list), payments/upi-link all return 200 with correct payloads. Frontend flows verified end-to-end with admin login.
passed: Admin login (admin@businessflow.io/Admin@12345) works and lands on /app
PUT /api/business/profile returns clean JSON (no _id, no owner_id leakage) — bug fix confirmed
Frontend /app/business Save button shows 'Business profile saved' toast
GET /api/payments/settings returns default INR settings for existing user
PUT /api/payments/settings stores only last-4 of account number (sent 123456789 -> stored 6789)
POST /api/payments creates payment with auto-ref PAY-00001, PAY-00002 sequentially
Frontend /app/payments loads, sidebar 'Payments' link visible with rupee icon
Payments tabs (Received / Payment details) both functional
Record payment dialog creates payment and toast 'Payment recorded' shown
Generate UPI link opens dialog with QR image + copyable upi:// link
Dashboard KPIs and payments list display ₹ (Revenue ₹8,963.18, Profit ₹2,223.18, Expenses ₹6,740)

failed.issue: PUT /api/business/profile silently drops extended India fields (legal_name, gstin, pan, address_line1, city, state, pincode, country). Response only echoes base BusinessProfile fields (business_name, industry, email, phone, address, tax_id, currency, logo_url).
failed.evidence: curl PUT with full India body returned only base fields; extended fields not persisted or echoed.

failed.issue: Business profile currency defaults to 'USD' even though app is India-localized to INR. Consider defaulting currency to 'INR'.

action_items: Optional: extend BusinessProfile model to persist legal_name, gstin, pan, address_line1, city, state, pincode, country (currently accepted but dropped).
Optional: change default currency in BusinessProfile from USD to INR to match INR-focused seed data and UI.
